svg.title: Sixteen Disparate Views of Fibonacci Truth
svg.desc: A 4 by 4 SVG atlas under the repository Fv process-step convention. Each panel stays clipped to its own boundary and includes labels, axes, numbers, truth-status badges, and caveats distinguishing exact recurrence values, finite ratios, limits, geometry approximations, evidence boundaries, index conversion, and metaphor.
svg.text: SIXTEEN DISPARATE VIEWS OF FIBONACCI TRUTH
Fv process-step convention · exact values, finite ratios, limits, geometry, metaphor, and evidence boundaries kept separate

g.text: Do not omit Fv -1 or merge the duplicate destination 1.
Question: where does the repository address system begin?
The rule determines later formal terms; it does not imply real-world fate.
Linear bar scale, Fv -1..12. Early values are intentionally tiny.
Log transform makes multiplicative growth readable; data remain discrete.
Adjacent pairs expose ratio as slope: R_v = D_v / D_(v-1).
Finite R_v are rational; none equals φ exactly.
Finite Q_v are rational; none equals 1/φ exactly.
Signed error alternates around zero while shrinking in magnitude.
Higher means closer to φ; this is still finite approximation.
A φ rectangle is a reference model, not a finite Fibonacci ratio.
Squares 1,1,2,3,5,8,13; arcs are a Fibonacci spiral approximation.
Not an exact logarithmic golden spiral.
Markers are actual integer-step observations; dashed links show order only.
Interpretive coordinate; unequal distances around today.
Rule: never promote resemblance or metaphor into proof.
Repository Fv step → conventional index → destination
Question answered: which truths are different kinds?

svg.text: Caption: Values use the repository Fv convention. Exact recurrence, finite computation, limiting behavior, geometry approximation, evidence status, and metaphor are deliberately separated.
